tool_args.label: Central Business District Tolling Program
tool_args.raw_text: B&T continues its work on the Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP), which aims to reduce congestion in Manhattan's Central Business District (CBD) and generate sufficient net revenue to support $15 billion for the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program and subsequent capital programs.

payload.description: Aims to reduce congestion in Manhattan's Central Business District and generate net revenue to support $15 billion for the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program. Will impose a toll for vehicles entering or remaining in the CBD, defined as Manhattan south of and inclusive of 60th Street, not including the FDR Drive or the West Side Highway.
payload.status: ongoing - awaiting FHWA determination on Environmental Assessment

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: CBDTP FHWA Timeline Claim
tool_args.raw_text: While the goal was to implement the program in 2021, the MTA did not receive approval from FHWA to proceed with the federally required EA until March 2021.

tool_args.label: CBDTP FONSI Process Description
tool_args.raw_text: If a favorable decision is issued by FHWA, a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) will be issued and the notice to proceed to our contractor can follow. The contractor will have up to 310 days to complete the design, development, installation, and testing, and then commence toll collection.

payload.claim_text: If a favorable decision is issued by FHWA, a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) will be issued and the notice to proceed to our contractor can follow. The contractor will have up to 310 days to complete the design, development, installation, and testing, and then commence toll collection.
